programmes.show_description: The pianist Alexandre Kantorow performs Liszt's second piano concerto. Joined by the WDR Symphony Orchestra, who perform Schumann's Rhenish Symphony and Wagner's Faust Overture.
Tom McKinney presents Radio 3's Breakfast show. To get in touch, email 3breakfast@bbc.co.uk.
Georgia Mann plays the best in classical music, with discoveries and surprises rubbing shoulders with familiar favourites.
Mark Forrest introduces specially recorded performances from Perth of chamber music evoking the night and concludes his plainsong-inspired theme with a symphony by Rachmaninov.
5/5. Donald Macleod explores the origins and evolution of George Gershwin's opera Porgy and Bess.
Petroc introduces live music from the Urban Folk Quartet and pianist Hanni Liang.
Take time out with a 30-minute soundscape of classical favourites.
Jérôme Kuhn conducts the BBC Concert Orchestra in music which features American songs from soprano Alison Scherzer. Recorded at Chichester Festival Theatre in January.
Kate Kennedy hears from young musicians Rebecca Toal and Hattie Butterworth how mental health issues forced them to rethink their  relationship with their instruments.
Join Verity Sharp as she takes a spin around the world of experimental listening, from the bellows of church organs to the existential questioning of a virtual assistant.
Tonight, Soweto is joined by leading British jazz saxophonist, multi-instrumentalist and composer Courtney Pine, as his Friday night guest.
The Minetti Quartet and pianist Kit Armstrong perform music by Schubert, Schumann and Mozart. John Shea presents.
Hannah French presents Radio 3's Breakfast show. To get in touch, email 3breakfast@bbc.co.uk.
Tom Service with the perfect classical soundtrack and an all-star morning of guests, including Isata Kanneh-Mason, Sir Mark Rylance, Peter Dawkins and John Wilson.
Jools is joined by the author and film producer Olivia Harrison.
...Essential History of Classical Music. In the 1150s, the King of the Troubadours meets Queen Eleanor of Aquitaine.
The Observer's music critic Fiona Maddocks's pick of the week's new releases, and Nicholas Kenyon recommends a top recording of Haydn's Matin, Midi and Soir symphonies.
Your weekly journey through outstanding film scores and cinematic soundtracks, hosted by Edith Bowman - featuring Brian Irvine on his music to the documentary film The Flats.
Jess swaps favourite music with singer songwriter and co-founder of the Pet Shop Boys Neil Tennant.
From the Metropolitan Opera in New York, Mozart's Don Giovanni starring Ryan Speedo Green in the title role and Federica Lombardi as Donna Anna. Yannick Nézet-Séguin conducts.
Kathryn Tickell celebrates spring with folk ballads from Seamus Cater and Fredrik Rasten, Ethiopian music from saxophonist Tilaye Gebre, and currulao by Lyon-based Pixvae.
Tom Service presents the UK premiere of Daniel Bjarnason's first major symphonic work, I Want to be Alive.  Plus the latest in cutting-edge and experimental new music.
